title: vLookApp - A Free vLookup Function for Monday
excerpt:
- What is your app called? - vLookApp. 
- Why did you build this app? - Because I needed this tool. 
- What does your app do? - Exactly what it says, a Lookup function across Monday boards. 
- What example use cases and …

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
- What is your app called? - vLookApp.
- Why did you build this app? - Because I needed this tool.
- What does your app do? - Exactly what it says, a Lookup function across Monday boards.
- What example use cases and workflows does this app apply to? - If you need to lookup a value from one column in a different columns, and return a specific value from this row (just like in other spreadsheets).
- Who are you? Are you a monday partner? My name is Yogev, and I’m not sure what a Monday Partner is.
- How can users use your app? - Just install it here and follow the recipe explanation bellow.
- Are there any dependencies (eg: must have an enterprise subscription to X tool) - No.
- Does it have a cost? - No.

Here’s an explanation on how to use the recipe:

image2580×971 168 KB

Hope you’d find it useful,
I’m here for any questions,
You can also reach me at Yogev@formrally.com

posts.cookedHtml:
Sure thing, here’s a working example with a recipe:

image3830×1472 374 KB

posts.author: jared.smith (Jared Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Howdy! Just installed this, but am having trouble accessing it within the automations view. Is there a certain set of steps I need to take to find the vlookup functionality? Thanks!

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jared.smith !
It’s under “Integration” not “Automation”

image1172×367 18.5 KB

posts.author: Paolaura (Laura Delgadillo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie
I’m not sure if I’m doing it right:

Look up a value in client SKU in Artwork, column (Name), Return value from Status and mirror it to Approval

Artwork is the board where my client is a guest and confirms via a status column when each tag is approved or under review. I want that response to show up in a board where my colleagues are (Approval column) so they can see as soon as the same tag is approved within that second board.
The column Name is the first column from the left, I don’t see any other way to reference it. Nothing is showing up.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Paolaura !

Unfortunately, what you refer to as “the first column from the left” in not actually a column, it’s the Item Name, this is how Monday boards are designed, unlike in an excel spreadsheet.
Notice that you cannot move or delete it, for the exact same reason.

You can use this app with any other regular column though.
Hope this helps.

posts.author: Paolaura (Laura Delgadillo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you, @Yoggie!

If I were to move it to the second column rather than trying to use the first, what would I need to correct?
I think I am interpreting the recipe wrong.

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Workaround.

You can use a formula column to have h to e item name duplicated into it and link that.

posts.author: jjay.lewin (Jake Jay-Lewin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Yoggie I love the app! However, is there a way to have the “column” that contains the value I want to look up/look for to be the Item’s name?

same for the target I am looking it up in? Like, I want to use it for a central database of clients, so when I make a new board for a specific event that some clients might be interested in then I can just copy in their names and it instantly mirrors in all the relevant contact details.

Regards,
Jake

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jjay.lewin !

Just use a formula column and mirror the item’s name:

image1179×1214 52.9 KB

Hope this helps.

posts.cookedHtml: Hey Yoggie. Thanks for helping me set this up! Very useful app

posts.author: EA8 (Eddy)
posts.cookedHtml:
image2216×1396 185 KB

Hi Yoggie, it seems formulas columns are not supported ?

posts.author: EA8 (Eddy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m trying to have Vlookup replace the mirrors columns of monday, I though I could use the link board column and then use Vlookup to update the status of my columns (instead of having mirrors columns). However I see that the linkboard column is also not supported (VL1 in my screenshot) :

image2144×1320 178 KB

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @EA8 !

Unfortunately Monday don’t store the value of a formula, so automations and integrations do not apply to it.
They might change it in the future, the app is set to be able to handle function columns once they do.

2894×236 27.5 KB

posts.author: mmalone (Mariah)
posts.cookedHtml: I think I may not be correctly using this app, but I have a list of companies on a source board that trickles info down to the low-level boards but the item names are what I need to match to in the target boards (client names) and I am unable to do so… do I have to make the item name a short text in the target boards (without using a formula) ?

posts.author: Leon (Leon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie
I was looking for a way to facilitate forms data entry for our users. But found out that integrations nor automations are applicable to forms.
So what I’m trying to do now is, when new item is created on a board, I want to lookup the name of the Requestor on the same board, and mirror Requestor’s phone number in the new item’s phone column.
Basically, if same Requestor’s name already on a board with phone number column value already available, I want to return this phone column’s value to a new row.
Is it possible to do that with your App?

I tried to do it but when I add a new item, once I enter Requestor name, the integration takes the null value from phone column of the new row, and updates phone of existing rows of the same Requestor to null.
I need it to do opposite thing. Take phone value from existing row and update new row with it.
How do I do it?

My current integration is:

Look Up a Value in Requestor in Test Summary, Requestor, Return Value from Phone and Mirror it to Phone

Thanks.

posts.author: kathonmonday (kathonmonday)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @yoggie I’m trying to use this to assign someone in a people column, matching to a people column in another board where the “Job #” text column matches between the 2 boards. It’s not working. Are people columns supported? I can’t figure out what I might be doing wrong, but Monday’s mirror columns are not sufficient for notifications/automations and I need a way to match/automate assigning a person in a new board based on other boards.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @kathonmonday

I can’t answer for @Yoggie but supporting all different column types requires serious work for the app developers. This app https://monday.com/marketplace/129 supports the People column (and a lot more) and is triggered on changes in the source value and changes in the key (“Job #”) in any of the two boards.

posts.author: kathonmonday (kathonmonday)
posts.cookedHtml:
@basdebruin thanks! I looked into that but I’m trying to limit further third party integrations with $$ as I don’t have access to purchasing directly and have to make a really strong case for it. I just had our general caster upgraded, so I don’t think there’s a chance for that one. That app also looks like the cost is per lookup and I would need to employ it across several boards so I just see the costs going up and up.

I did figure out a workaround using general caster to cast the mirrored person to a dropdown column, and then creating an automation assigning a person based on the drop down column. It’s not as “dynamic” and requires an automation sequence per user, but it gets the job done for now.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @kathonmonday
Understood :), that is probably the difference between free and paid apps

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @kathonmonday,
Yeah the free version doesn’t support all column types yet, you can use a formula to duplicate the user column as a work around.
I know it worked for other users.

posts.author: lucidmattnj (Matt D)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie ,

vLookup is great, and seems to be solving a number of issues I’ve been having. One thing is still nagging though, and I may just be missing something. Am I able to use vLookup to pull data from multiple boards into one column? For instance, I have low-level-1 and low-level-2 boards with timelines. However when I try to create a high level and link lowl-level-1 and low-level-2’s timelines with a high level timeline, it appears that only one source board can feed into a particular column.

is that correct or am I missing an option to add multiple sources to a single column (the correct board being selected by the sourceid)?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lucidmattnj !

Actually I don’t think you missed anything, perhaps you need the apps’s full version that supports more complex use-cases.

Feel free to contact me here.

Cheers
